content:
Google has introduced TurboQuant, a breakthrough model compression technique that reduces transformer key-value cache size to just 3 bits per value without any accuracy loss or need for retraining. It works by innovatively combining two quantization methods to minimize memory use while maintaining performance in large language models.  The approach enables faster inference and significantly reduces GPU memory demands during model execution.

• TurboQuant compresses key-value cache to 3 bits without training or accuracy degradation  
• It combines PolarQuant and QJL to eliminate the need for extra correction constants in quantization  
• PolarQuant transforms data into a predictable coordinate system for efficient compression  
• QJL adds a 1-bit correction to remove bias, enabling high accuracy with minimal overhead
